## Codebase Patterns
- In this repository, run both `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` before committing feature work.
- Convex schema in this codebase uses snake_case field names and numeric `*_at` timestamps, with explicit `by_*` index naming.
- For third-party integrations, keep reusable helpers in `lib/` and expose credential readers for both `process.env` and Convex `ctx.env.get(...)`.
- For externally shared links, resolve hostnames through `lib/appBaseUrl.ts`; `APP_BASE_URL` is required and normalized to an HTTPS origin (no fallback host).
- For admin auth in this codebase, keep credential verification in a Convex action and consume it from NextAuth Credentials provider (`lib/auth.ts`) so role/username live in JWT session callbacks.
- For role-based button visibility, check `session.user.role === 'super_admin'` from NextAuth session on server-side before rendering edit/cancel buttons.
- Cancelled sessions must be excluded from the terms acceptance session selector dropdown by filtering `status !== 'cancelled'`.
- Class cancellation requires validation: check for active future sessions (`status !== 'cancelled' AND date >= today`) before allowing cancellation.
- Keep persistent admin navigation in `app/admin/layout.tsx` and keep route highlighting logic in a client-side `app/admin/admin-nav.tsx` component.
- For local Playwright auth flows with NextAuth JWT sessions, start dev server with a fixed `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` to avoid `JWT_SESSION_ERROR` decryption failures.
- When Convex function signatures change, run `npx convex dev --once` before local browser checks so Next.js and direct test calls both use the updated backend contract.
- In class lifecycle flows, treat cancelled classes as `classes.status = "inactive"` (schema-level enum), and use UI labels/badges to present that state as "cancelled".

## 2026-03-05 15:49:09 UTC - US-001
Thread: https://ampcode.com/threads/$AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID
- Implemented fail-fast APP_BASE_URL resolution for terms links by removing the hardcoded fallback host and normalizing inputs to canonical HTTPS origins.
- Verified terms-link behavior via targeted Playwright checks (`TC-012` through `TC-015`), including hostname consistency and tokenized absolute URL format.
- Files changed: `lib/appBaseUrl.ts`, `prd.json`, `progress.txt`
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered: link-building helpers should strip path segments from configured base URLs and use origin-only output to avoid accidental `/frontend/.../terms` URLs.
  - Gotchas encountered: running the full Playwright suite currently surfaces unrelated admin-nav/action failures, so scope-targeted tests are needed to validate this hostname story.
- Useful context: `resolveAppBaseUrl` is consumed by purchase confirmation and participant link actions, so APP_BASE_URL validation affects both flows.
---
## 2026-03-05 15:57:23 UTC - US-002
Thread: https://ampcode.com/threads/$AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID
- Implemented persistent admin navigation by adding `AdminNav` and wiring it through `app/admin/layout.tsx` so navigation remains visible across admin routes.
- Added top-level admin routes for `Sessions`, `Participants`, and `Terms` to ensure all nav links resolve and can be used for cross-page navigation.
- Added active-route highlighting with `aria-current="page"` and semantic `<nav>` + anchor links for accessibility.
- Added focused browser verification test `e2e/tc002-admin-nav.spec.ts` and executed it successfully against local dev server.
- Added shared e2e helper `e2e/qa-utils.ts` and minimal placeholder edit routes expected by generated type validators.
- Files changed: `app/admin/layout.tsx`, `app/admin/admin-nav.tsx`, `app/admin/sessions/page.tsx`, `app/admin/participants/page.tsx`, `app/admin/terms/page.tsx`, `app/admin/classes/[class_id]/edit/page.tsx`, `app/admin/sessions/[session_id]/edit/page.tsx`, `e2e/tc002-admin-nav.spec.ts`, `e2e/qa-utils.ts`, `prd.json`, `progress.txt`
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered: for admin-wide UI chrome, prefer placing shared components in `app/admin/layout.tsx` so nested routes inherit behavior consistently.
  - Gotchas encountered: NextAuth JWT session decoding in local dev is unstable without fixed `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`; Playwright login checks should launch dev with explicit auth env vars.
- Useful context: current nav route coverage includes `/admin/dashboard`, `/admin/classes`, `/admin/sessions`, `/admin/participants`, and `/admin/terms`, with active state computed from pathname.
---
## 2026-03-05 16:03:16 UTC - US-003
Thread: https://ampcode.com/threads/$AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID
- Implemented super-admin class editing on `/admin/classes` with row-level `Edit` actions that open a modal prefilled with current class name and description.
- Added `adminClasses:updateClass` mutation with server-side super-admin authorization and audit logging, then wired server action handling and success/error states in the classes page.
- Added browser verification test `e2e/tc003-class-edit.spec.ts` and shared query helper usage to confirm updated values persist and render immediately after save.
- Files changed: `app/admin/classes/page.tsx`, `app/admin/classes/edit-class-modal.tsx`, `convex/adminClasses.ts`, `e2e/qa-utils.ts`, `e2e/tc003-class-edit.spec.ts`, `prd.json`, `progress.txt`
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered: class-row actions that mutate data should be implemented as client modal components receiving a server action, matching existing add-modal behavior.
  - Gotchas encountered: local browser tests against Convex-backed pages can fail silently on stale function contracts until `npx convex dev --once` publishes the new query/mutation signatures.
- Useful context: `adminClasses:getClassListPageData` now returns optional `description`, enabling prefilled edit forms without extra per-row fetches.
---
## 2026-03-05 16:07:58 UTC - US-004
Thread: https://ampcode.com/threads/$AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID
- Implemented class cancellation end-to-end: added super-admin-only `Cancel` action per class row, confirmation dialog before submit, success/error messaging, and cancelled visual treatment (grayed row + cancelled badge).
- Added server-side `adminClasses:cancelClass` mutation with guard validation to block cancellation when the class has active future sessions (`session.status !== "cancelled"` and `session.date >= today`), plus audit logging.
- Added browser verification spec that covers both guard failure and successful cancellation flows.
- Files changed: `app/admin/classes/page.tsx`, `app/admin/classes/cancel-class-button.tsx`, `convex/adminClasses.ts`, `e2e/tc004-class-cancel.spec.ts`, `prd.json`, `progress.txt`
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered: use row-scoped client form components for destructive actions in server-rendered tables so confirmation UI stays local while action execution remains server-side.
  - Gotchas encountered: default Playwright config targets production unless `BASE_URL` is overridden, so browser verification for in-progress features must run against local dev (`BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:<port>`).
  - Useful context: class cancellation persistence currently maps to `classes.status = "inactive"` in Convex, with guard logic implemented in `adminClasses:cancelClass`.
---

import { defineSchema, defineTable } from "convex/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";

export default defineSchema({
  purchases: defineTable({
    order_id: v.string(),
    customer_mobile: v.string(),
    purchase_datetime: v.string(),
    participant_count: v.number(),
    status: v.union(
      v.literal("pending_terms"),
      v.literal("confirmation_sent"),
      v.literal("terms_accepted"),
      v.literal("cancelled")
    ),
    token: v.string(),
    class_id: v.optional(v.string()),
    session_id: v.optional(v.string()),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_order_id", ["order_id"])
    .index("by_token", ["token"]),

  participants: defineTable({
    participant_id: v.string(),
    purchase_id: v.id("purchases"),
    session_id: v.string(),
    name: v.optional(v.string()),
    mobile: v.optional(v.string()),
    qr_code_data: v.optional(v.string()),
    terms_accepted_at: v.optional(v.number()),
    terms_version_id: v.optional(v.id("terms_versions")),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_participant_id", ["participant_id"])
    .index("by_session_id", ["session_id"]),

  classes: defineTable({
    class_id: v.string(),
    name: v.string(),
    description: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.union(v.literal("active"), v.literal("inactive")),
    created_at: v.number(),
  }).index("by_class_id", ["class_id"]),

  sessions: defineTable({
    session_id: v.string(),
    class_id: v.string(),
    location: v.string(),
    date: v.string(),
    time: v.string(),
    quota_defined: v.number(),
    quota_used: v.number(),
    status: v.union(
      v.literal("scheduled"),
      v.literal("completed"),
      v.literal("cancelled")
    ),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_session_id", ["session_id"])
    .index("by_class_id", ["class_id"]),

  terms_versions: defineTable({
    version: v.string(),
    content: v.string(),
    is_current: v.boolean(),
    created_at: v.number(),
  }).index("by_is_current", ["is_current"]),

  admins: defineTable({
    username: v.string(),
    role: v.union(v.literal("super_admin"), v.literal("regular_admin")),
    password_hash: v.string(),
    created_at: v.number(),
  }).index("by_username", ["username"]),

  audit_logs: defineTable({
    admin_id: v.optional(v.id("admins")),
    action: v.string(),
    entity_type: v.string(),
    entity_id: v.string(),
    metadata: v.optional(v.any()),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_admin_id", ["admin_id"])
    .index("by_entity", ["entity_type", "entity_id"]),

  csv_files: defineTable({
    filename: v.string(),
    file_storage_id: v.string(),
    status: v.union(
      v.literal("pending"),
      v.literal("processed"),
      v.literal("failed")
    ),
    processed_at: v.optional(v.number()),
    error_message: v.optional(v.string()),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_status", ["status"])
    .index("by_file_storage_id", ["file_storage_id"]),

  attendance_records: defineTable({
    attendance_id: v.string(),
    participant_id: v.string(),
    session_id: v.string(),
    marked_by_admin: v.id("admins"),
    marked_at: v.number(),
    created_at: v.number(),
  })
    .index("by_attendance_id", ["attendance_id"])
    .index("by_participant_id", ["participant_id"])
    .index("by_session_id", ["session_id"]),
});

{
  "name": "class-management-system",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "npx playwright test"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "bcryptjs": "^3.0.3",
    "convex": "^1.32.0",
    "next": "16.1.6",
    "next-auth": "^4.24.13",
    "qr-scanner": "^1.4.2",
    "qrcode": "^1.5.4",
    "react": "19.2.3",
    "react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "twilio": "^5.12.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "^1.58.2",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4",
    "@types/node": "^20",
    "@types/qrcode": "^1.5.6",
    "@types/react": "^19",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19",
    "eslint": "^9",
    "eslint-config-next": "16.1.6",
    "tailwindcss": "^4",
    "typescript": "^5"
  }
}

item.text: I have enough context and I’m implementing `US-005` now by mirroring the proven class-edit pattern: add a super-admin-only session edit modal in the class sessions table, add a server mutation with role checks, wire redirect statuses/messages, and add a focused Playwright verification spec.

I’m now editing four areas: `adminSessions` mutation layer, the class-session page server actions/UI, a new `EditSessionModal` client component, and a focused Playwright test for the edit flow. After that I’ll run Convex codegen/typecheck/lint/tests.
I’ve added the backend mutation and modal component; next I’m wiring the server action plus row-level button visibility and success/error handling into the session list page.

For browser verification, I’m using the `playwright` skill (the requested `dev-browser` skill isn’t available in this workspace). I’ll read its `SKILL.md` and then run the required local browser check for this frontend story.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: "playwright"
description: "Use when the task requires automating a real browser from the terminal (navigation, form filling, snapshots, screenshots, data extraction, UI-flow debugging) via `playwright-cli` or the bundled wrapper script."
---


# Playwright CLI Skill

Drive a real browser from the terminal using `playwright-cli`. Prefer the bundled wrapper script so the CLI works even when it is not globally installed.
Treat this skill as CLI-first automation. Do not pivot to `@playwright/test` unless the user explicitly asks for test files.

## Prerequisite check (required)

Before proposing commands, check whether `npx` is available (the wrapper depends on it):

```bash
command -v npx >/dev/null 2>&1
```

If it is not available, pause and ask the user to install Node.js/npm (which provides `npx`). Provide these steps verbatim:

```bash
# Verify Node/npm are installed
node --version
npm --version

# If missing, install Node.js/npm, then:
npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest
playwright-cli --help
```

Once `npx` is present, proceed with the wrapper script. A global install of `playwright-cli` is optional.

## Skill path (set once)

```bash
export CODEX_HOME="${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}"
export PWCLI="$CODEX_HOME/skills/playwright/scripts/playwright_cli.sh"
```

User-scoped skills install under `$CODEX_HOME/skills` (default: `~/.codex/skills`).

## Quick start

Use the wrapper script:

```bash
"$PWCLI" open https://playwright.dev --headed
"$PWCLI" snapshot
"$PWCLI" click e15
"$PWCLI" type "Playwright"
"$PWCLI" press Enter
"$PWCLI" screenshot
```

If the user prefers a global install, this is also valid:

```bash
npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest
playwright-cli --help
```

## Core workflow

1. Open the page.
2. Snapshot to get stable element refs.
3. Interact using refs from the latest snapshot.
4. Re-snapshot after navigation or significant DOM changes.
5. Capture artifacts (screenshot, pdf, traces) when useful.

Minimal loop:

```bash
"$PWCLI" open https://example.com
"$PWCLI" snapshot
"$PWCLI" click e3
"$PWCLI" snapshot
```

## When to snapshot again

Snapshot again after:

- navigation
- clicking elements that change the UI substantially
- opening/closing modals or menus
- tab switches

Refs can go stale. When a command fails due to a missing ref, snapshot again.

## Recommended patterns

### Form fill and submit

```bash
"$PWCLI" open https://example.com/form
"$PWCLI" snapshot
"$PWCLI" fill e1 "user@example.com"
"$PWCLI" fill e2 "password123"
"$PWCLI" click e3
"$PWCLI" snapshot
```

### Debug a UI flow with traces

```bash
"$PWCLI" open https://example.com --headed
"$PWCLI" tracing-start
# ...interactions...
"$PWCLI" tracing-stop
```

### Multi-tab work

```bash
"$PWCLI" tab-new https://example.com
"$PWCLI" tab-list
"$PWCLI" tab-select 0
"$PWCLI" snapshot
```

## Wrapper script

The wrapper script uses `npx --package @playwright/cli playwright-cli` so the CLI can run without a global install:

```bash
"$PWCLI" --help
```

Prefer the wrapper unless the repository already standardizes on a global install.

## References

Open only what you need:

- CLI command reference: `references/cli.md`
- Practical workflows and troubleshooting: `references/workflows.md`

## Guardrails

- Always snapshot before referencing element ids like `e12`.
- Re-snapshot when refs seem stale.
- Prefer explicit commands over `eval` and `run-code` unless needed.
- When you do not have a fresh snapshot, use placeholder refs like `eX` and say why; do not bypass refs with `run-code`.
- Use `--headed` when a visual check will help.
- When capturing artifacts in this repo, use `output/playwright/` and avoid introducing new top-level artifact folders.
- Default to CLI commands and workflows, not Playwright test specs.

item.text: I’m now updating project tracking files: marking `US-005` as passing in `prd.json`, adding one reusable pattern at the top of `progress.txt`, and appending this iteration’s implementation log entry.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "cat <<'EOF' >> progress.txt
## 2026-03-05 16:12:51 UTC - US-005
Thread: https://ampcode.com/threads/"'$AMP_CURRENT_THREAD_ID
- Implemented super-admin session editing on the class session list page with row-level `Edit` actions that open a modal prefilled with current location, date, time, and quota.
- Added `adminSessions:updateSession` mutation with server-side super-admin authorization, input validation, and audit logging; wired a server action in the page to persist updates and return status/error feedback.
- Added browser verification coverage via `e2e/tc005-session-edit.spec.ts`, including UI prefill checks, save flow, immediate UI refresh assertions, and backend data verification.
- Files changed: `app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/page.tsx`, `app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/edit-session-modal.tsx`, `convex/adminSessions.ts`, `e2e/tc005-session-edit.spec.ts`, `prd.json`, `progress.txt`
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered: for session-row mutations, match the existing class flow by combining a row-scoped client modal with a server action that redirects using `status` query params for success banners.
  - Gotchas encountered: assertions tied to old row text become stale after edits; locate updated rows using post-save values to keep Playwright checks stable.
  - Useful context: the actionable session list for admin work is under `/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions` (not the `/admin/sessions` landing page).
---
EOF'

item.text:
Implemented `US-005` on branch `ralph/bug-admin-nav-actions-hostname` and committed it.

Commit:
- `b8239c5` with message `feat: US-005 - Add edit button for sessions (super admin only)`

Key changes:
- Added super-admin-only session edit mutation with server-side auth + audit log:
  - [convex/adminSessions.ts](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/convex/adminSessions.ts)
- Added session edit modal (prefilled location/date/time/quota) and wired save flow:
  - [app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/edit-session-modal.tsx](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/edit-session-modal.tsx)
  - [app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/page.tsx](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/app/admin/classes/[class_id]/sessions/page.tsx)
- Added browser verification test for the full edit flow:
  - [e2e/tc005-session-edit.spec.ts](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/e2e/tc005-session-edit.spec.ts)
- Updated tracking:
  - [prd.json](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/prd.json)
  - [progress.txt](/home/ec2-user/.openclaw/workspace/class-management-system/progress.txt)

Quality checks run:
- `npx convex dev --once` passed
- `npm run typecheck` passed
- `npm run lint` passed with warnings only (no errors)
- Browser verification: `BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npx playwright test e2e/tc005-session-edit.spec.ts` passed

AGENTS updates:
- Checked for nearby `AGENTS.md` files; none exist in this repo, so no AGENTS update was needed.

Note:
- There are still stories with `passes: false` (`US-006`, `US-007`), so work is not complete yet.
